theory.canonical_statement: ID 441 establishes the surface finishing protocol for the internal torus walls. Surface roughness introduces dissipation and modifies the calculated Casimir forces. By employing Chemical Mechanical Polishing (CMP), the SS304L surfaces are brought to an atomically smooth state (Ra < 0.2 nm), which is essential for maintaining the "Stillness Buoyancy" of the vacuum manifold and reaching the UHV limit of 10 9 Pa. Friction Reduction: Eliminates microscopic surface asperities that cause parasitic drag on the rotating ferrite core. Flux Stability: Prevents non canonical scattering of the cym waves.
theory.technical_interpretation: ID 441 establishes the surface finishing protocol for the internal torus walls. Surface roughness introduces dissipation and modifies the calculated Casimir forces. By employing Chemical Mechanical Polishing (CMP), the SS304L surfaces are brought to an atomically smooth state (Ra < 0.2 nm), which is essential for maintaining the "Stillness Buoyancy" of the vacuum manifold and reaching the UHV limit of 10 9 Pa. Friction Reduction: Eliminates microscopic surface asperities that cause parasitic drag on the rotating ferrite core. Flux Stability: Prevents non canonical scattering of the cym waves.
